recipe.title: Humble chicken stew & dumplings
recipe.description: A simple delicious chicken stew done one of my favourite ways – with leftover chicken and fluffy dumplings. Enjoy!

ingredients.name: leftover free-range chicken carcass
ingredients.note: plus up to 300g leftover cooked chicken

ingredients.name: rashers of higher-welfare smoked streaky bacon
a few sprigs of fresh thyme

steps.descr: Strip all the meat you can find off the chicken carcass,...
steps.instructions: Strip all the meat you can find off the chicken carcass, and put aside.

on_enter.say: Strip all the meat you can find off the chicken carcass, and put aside.

steps.descr: Place the carcass and any bones in a large pan and use a...
steps.instructions: Place the carcass and any bones in a large pan and use a rolling pin to smash them all up. Cover with 1 litre of water, bring to the boil, then simmer for at least 30 minutes, skimming away any scum from the surface.

on_enter.say: Place the carcass and any bones in a large pan and use a rolling pin to smash them all up. Cover with 1 litre of water, bring to the boil, then simmer for at least 30 minutes, skimming away any scum from the surface.

steps.descr: Meanwhile, finely slice the bacon and place in a large...
steps.instructions: Meanwhile, finely slice the bacon and place in a large casserole pan on a medium heat with a lug of oil, while you peel and chop the onions, carrots, and potatoes or celeriac into 2cm dice.

on_enter.say: Meanwhile, finely slice the bacon and place in a large casserole pan on a medium heat with a lug of oil, while you peel and chop the onions, carrots, and potatoes or celeriac into 2cm dice.

steps.descr: Add to the pan along with the thyme sprigs and bay leaves
steps.instructions: Add to the pan along with the thyme sprigs and bay leaves. Cook for 10 minutes, stirring regularly.

on_enter.say: Add to the pan along with the thyme sprigs and bay leaves. Cook for 10 minutes, stirring regularly.

steps.descr: Halve and stir in the mushrooms, along with the leftover...
steps.instructions: Halve and stir in the mushrooms, along with the leftover chicken and plain flour.

on_enter.say: Halve and stir in the mushrooms, along with the leftover chicken and plain flour.

steps.descr: Pour the stock through a sieve straight into the pan...
steps.instructions: Pour the stock through a sieve straight into the pan (topping up with a little water, if needed) and let it simmer for 20 minutes, or until thick and delicious, while you start your dumplings.

on_enter.say: Pour the stock through a sieve straight into the pan (topping up with a little water, if needed) and let it simmer for 20 minutes, or until thick and delicious, while you start your dumplings.

steps.descr: Place the self-raising flour and a pinch of sea salt in...
steps.instructions: Place the self-raising flour and a pinch of sea salt in a mixing bowl. Coarsely grate in the butter and rub together until it resembles breadcrumbs.

on_enter.say: Place the self-raising flour and a pinch of sea salt in a mixing bowl. Coarsely grate in the butter and rub together until it resembles breadcrumbs.

steps.descr: Add 100ml of cold water and bring into a ball of dough
steps.instructions: Add 100ml of cold water and bring into a ball of dough. Divide into 12 pieces and roll into balls.

on_enter.say: Add 100ml of cold water and bring into a ball of dough. Divide into 12 pieces and roll into balls.

steps.descr: When the time’s up, loosen the stew with a good splash...
steps.instructions: When the time’s up, loosen the stew with a good splash of water if needed, season to perfection, then transfer it to an ovenproof pan and place the balls on top.

on_enter.say: When the time’s up, loosen the stew with a good splash of water if needed, season to perfection, then transfer it to an ovenproof pan and place the balls on top.

steps.descr: Pop the lid on and bake at 190°C/375°F/gas 5 for 30...
steps.instructions: Pop the lid on and bake at 190°C/375°F/gas 5 for 30 minutes, or until hot through and the dumplings are fluffy and cooked. Serve with some seasonal greens.

on_enter.say: Pop the lid on and bake at 190°C/375°F/gas 5 for 30 minutes, or until hot through and the dumplings are fluffy and cooked. Serve with some seasonal greens.
